subject: mathematics

core_competencies.justification: The chapter's primary operation is classifying and understanding 2D shapes through their defining symmetry properties. Sections 9.1 and 9.2 systematically classify shapes — triangles, quadrilaterals, regular polygons, the circle — by their number of lines of symmetry and angles of rotational symmetry, and exercises throughout (E-1 through E-17, E-18 through E-31) require students to describe and distinguish shapes using these properties.

Section 9.2 and exercises E-28–E-31 apply properties of lines, angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons to determine symmetry: the chapter derives the 360°/n formula for regular n-arm figures and applies properties of regular polygons to count their lines and angles of symmetry, constituting a distinct second strand beyond general shape classification.

adjunct_competencies.justification: Activity A-6 (Playing with Tiles) and exercises E-11 (tracing figures with symmetry on grids), E-12 (finding symmetry in a kolam), and E-21 (colouring circle sectors for symmetric designs) substantively engage tiling and decorative design in the Indian cultural tradition, directly connecting symmetry to art forms such as kolam, rangoli, and tile patterns.

Exercises E-13 (draw triangles with exactly 1, 3, or 0 lines of symmetry), E-14 (draw curved figures with specified line counts), E-22 (draw non-standard figures with both symmetry types), and E-23 (sketch quadrilaterals with specified symmetry combinations) require students to generate novel figures that meet given constraints — creative problem-solving that goes beyond identification.

Section 9.2 develops the generalisation that all angles of symmetry are multiples of the smallest angle and derives the 360°/n formula through structured inductive-then-deductive reasoning; exercises E-24 through E-26 and the True/False questions require students to apply this logical chain to determine angles of symmetry and evaluate claims about rotational symmetry.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to describe and classify 2D shapes by identifying, reasoning about, and constructing their lines of symmetry and angles of rotational symmetry.
